title: Pump Set (Fluid Administration)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Administration of intravenous fluids/medication where rapid infusion is required. 

#### Procedure

- Remove fluid from outer bag and check for discolouration or sedimentation.

- Confirm “5 rights” with paramedic partner, **the right**: 
- Patient
- Drug
- Dose
- Time
- Route
- Remove pump set from pack and reposition drip regulator near fluid chamber and turn off the roller regulator.

- Remove protective cap from both fluid bag and perforator, taking care not to contaminate either. Discard should contamination occur.

- Carefully insert perforator into fluid bag port taking care not to puncture the port wall.

- Suspend the bag and squeeze the lower half of the drip chamber until half full.

- Invert the pump chamber and open the drip regulator. Once the chamber is full of fluid, it may be released. Continue flow until all air is expelled from the line then close the regulator.

- Remove protective cap and insert the end of the line into the cannula. Secure by tightening the rotating collar.

- Secure the IV line to the patient as appropriate with tape, armlock or bandage.

#### Specific Notes

- Pump set can hold 50-60mls of fluid. Beware when calculating doses.
- Control flow rate by toggling roller regulator.
- Regularly reassess for dislodgment, swelling, reddening or discomfort.
- Always ensure medication pre administration checks are completed prior to any drug administration.
